principles.name: Reciprocity
principles.description: People feel obligated to return favors. Give value before asking.
implementations: Free trial with no credit card
ux_patterns: Show free value above the fold before any ask
Interactive demo that gives results before signup
audit_questions: Does the user receive something before being asked to pay/signup?
Is the free value genuinely useful (not just a teaser)?
Is the give-before-ask ratio at least 3:1?

principles.name: Scarcity
principles.description: Limited availability drives urgency and perceived value.
ux_patterns: Countdown timer in hero or pricing section
audit_questions: Is the scarcity genuine (not manufactured)?
Is the urgency clearly communicated?
Does the scarcity element have high visual weight?

principles.name: Authority
principles.description: People trust experts and established credentials.
ux_patterns: Author authority box on content pages
audit_questions: Are authority signals visible above the fold?
Do endorsements include specific credentials?
Are logos from recognizable brands?

principles.name: Consistency
principles.description: People want to act consistently with prior commitments.
ux_patterns: Quiz that leads to personalized recommendation
audit_questions: Is the first commitment small and easy?
Does each step feel like a natural progression?
Is there a progress indicator showing investment?

principles.name: Liking
principles.description: People are influenced by those they like and relate to.
ux_patterns: About section with real team photos
audit_questions: Does the brand feel like a likeable person?
Is there a human story or face visible?
Does the copy feel warm, not corporate?

principles.name: Social Proof
principles.description: People look to others' actions to determine their own.
ux_patterns: Real-time notification toasts ('X just signed up')
audit_questions: Are testimonials from real, named people?
Do social proof numbers feel specific (not rounded)?
Is social proof visible near decision points (CTA, pricing)?

principles.name: Unity
principles.description: Shared identity creates powerful bonds and belonging.
ux_patterns: Mission banner connecting brand to cause
audit_questions: Does the visitor feel like they belong to a group?
Is the shared identity clearly stated?
Does joining create a sense of exclusivity?

loss_aversion.description: People feel losses 2x more than equivalent gains. Frame headlines around what users LOSE by not acting.

transformation_examples.after: Stop losing 53% of visitors to slow loading
Stop bleeding traffic to competitors who rank above you
You're throwing away $2,400/year on tools you don't use
Your team wastes 12 hours/week on tasks AI can handle

anchoring.description: The first number a person sees becomes their reference point. Always show the higher number first.
examples: Lead with user count: 'Used by 500+ Fortune 500 companies'
Show time saved: 'Save 40 hours/month (that's a full work week)'
